summary: Fabric v1.1 unified feature rollout: Dashboard (v1.1 Feature #5, D1-D5 + E1-E3) and Doc-Init extension (Rec#1-6). Dashboard introduces a single-process Express app ('fab serve') hosting MCP HTTP (per-session factory), Dashboard REST/SSE API, and a Preact+Vite SPA with 3 views (rules-tree / human-lock / intent-timeline). Doc-Init extends 'fab init' to produce forensic.json evidence, install agents-md-init SKILL.md + Stop hook, and drive a 3-Phase AI-assisted AGENTS.md initialization workflow. All 15 tasks are organized in 3 waves: Wave A (foundations, parallel), Wave B (server + dashboard, sequential), Wave C (polish + extensions, mostly parallel).
approach: Parallel-interleaved 3-wave execution. Wave A (TASK-001 to TASK-005): shared schema migration as the unblocking foundation; Doc-Init Rec#1-3 chained sequentially; UI design pre-stage via /ui-ux-pro-max skill runs in parallel. Wave B (TASK-006 to TASK-009): MCP HTTP + fab serve first, then REST API and SSE in parallel, then Preact SPA merging all three Wave B prerequisites plus UI design. Wave C (TASK-010 to TASK-015): tests, template refactor, docs, and E1-E3 optional extensions run in parallel after their respective Wave A/B predecessors complete. Zero new npm dependencies for HTTP layer (MCP SDK v1.29 provides Hono+Express transitively). Non-destructive principle throughout Doc-Init writes.
estimated_time: 6-8 days of focused implementation (Wave A: ~2 days, Wave B: ~2.5 days, Wave C: ~1.5 days)

design_decisions.decision: MCP SDK v1.29 createMcpExpressApp for zero-dependency HTTP layer
design_decisions.rationale: SDK already carries Hono+Express as transitive deps; built-in DNS rebinding protection and OAuth scaffolding
design_decisions.tradeoff: Locks HTTP implementation to SDK's express adapter; custom middleware must be compatible with SDK's Express v5 instance

design_decisions.rationale: SDK connect() takes exclusive transport ownership; factory pattern aligns with existing createFabricServer() pure function
design_decisions.tradeoff: Each HTTP MCP session creates a new server object; acceptable for single-user local tool

design_decisions.rationale: Preserves 'dual-arbiter' semantic; enables dual-column Dashboard timeline; mirrors async arbitration positioning
design_decisions.tradeoff: Requires backward-compat handling for existing ledger records without source field (defaults to 'human')

design_decisions.decision: Ritually-minimal write model: 2 write endpoints only (human-lock/approve + intent/annotate)
design_decisions.rationale: Prevents Dashboard from competing with CLI/IDE for file ownership; avoids optimistic-lock complexity
design_decisions.tradeoff: AGENTS.md direct editing stays in IDE — Dashboard is intentionally read-heavy

design_decisions.rationale: 3KB bundle vs 40KB React; Vite DX; compatible with maintenance-tool body budget
design_decisions.tradeoff: Smaller ecosystem than React; component library choices more limited

design_decisions.decision: fab init single entry point (no new fab doc-init command)
design_decisions.rationale: Zero new CLI surface; non-destructive principle; AI-client auto-trigger via stdout reason text; non-AI fallback degrades gracefully
design_decisions.tradeoff: fab init now does more — evidence + protocol install + skill trigger — but each step is idempotent and non-destructive

design_decisions.decision: Sentinel-based Stop hook (Option C hybrid trigger)
design_decisions.rationale: forensic.json exists + init-context.json missing = initialization pending; covers 4 trigger scenarios without time-expiry issues
design_decisions.tradeoff: Stop hook fires on every session stop until init-context.json created — intentional but slightly noisy until initialization completes

design_decisions.decision: TASK-005 UI pre-stage via /ui-ux-pro-max before TASK-009 Preact implementation
design_decisions.rationale: Defines component contract (tokens.json + component-inventory.md) before coding begins; reduces visual backtracking during SPA implementation
design_decisions.tradeoff: Adds a pre-implementation design step; mitigated by clear convergence criteria on ui-design/ artifacts

data_flow.diagram: fab init → .fabric/ (forensic.json) → agents-md-init SKILL → .fabric/ (init-context.json) → AGENTS.md; fab serve → Express app → [/mcp: per-session McpServer] + [/api/*: services layer] + [/events: chokidar SSE] + [/: Preact SPA dist]

stages.output: @fabric/shared exports: AgentsMeta, LedgerEntry, HumanLockEntry, FabricConfig, ForensicReport, InitContext, FabricEvent
Server-Sent Events pushed to Dashboard clients

dimensions.completeness: passed — all D1-D5 + E1-E3 + Rec#1-6 covered across 15 tasks
dimensions.granularity: passed — each task estimated 15-90 min; D5 and TASK-009 are larger but represent single cohesive deliverables
dimensions.dependencies: passed — no circular deps; TASK-001 is the sole Wave A foundation; TASK-009 correctly merges 3 predecessors
dimensions.convergence_criteria: passed — all tasks have quantified, testable criteria (curl responses, file assertions, pnpm build pass)
dimensions.implementation_steps: passed — all tasks have 4+ actionable implementation steps
dimensions.constraint_compliance: passed — zero new npm deps for HTTP, non-destructive pattern throughout, LedgerEntry backward compat, ≤300 line AGENTS.md
